1→# Emdash CMS: Comprehensive How-To Guide
     2→
     3→> A practical guide for building and managing sites with Emdash CMS, written for the Shipyard AI team.
     4→
     5→Emdash is a full-stack TypeScript CMS built on [Astro](https://astro.build/) and [Cloudflare](https://www.cloudflare.com/). It takes the ideas that made WordPress dominant -- extensibility, admin UX, a plugin ecosystem -- and rebuilds them on serverless, type-safe foundations. Plugins run in sandboxed Worker isolates, solving the fundamental security problem with WordPress's plugin architecture.
     6→
     7→**Key characteristics:**
     8→
     9→- **Astro-native** -- not a headless CMS; tightly integrated with Astro's rendering pipeline
    10→- **Database-first schema** -- collections and fields are defined in the database, not code
    11→- **Live Collections** -- content changes appear immediately, no static rebuilds needed
    12→- **Portable Text** -- structured JSON content format (not serialized HTML)
    13→- **Cloud-portable** -- runs on Cloudflare (Workers + D1 + R2), Node.js, SQLite, PostgreSQL, and any S3-compatible storage
    14→- **Sandboxed plugins** -- on Cloudflare, plugins run in isolated V8 Worker isolates with enforced capability restrictions
    15→
    16→---
    17→
    18→## Table of Contents
    19→
    20→1. [Getting Started](#1-getting-started)
    21→2. [Content Management](#2-content-management)
    22→3. [Admin Panel](#3-admin-panel)
    23→4. [Querying Content in Templates](#4-querying-content-in-templates)
    24→5. [Deployment](#5-deployment)
    25→6. [Plugin System](#6-plugin-system)
    26→7. [Theming](#7-theming)
    27→8. [Real Examples from Shipyard Sites](#8-real-examples-from-shipyard-sites)
    28→
    29→---
    30→
    31→## 1. Getting Started
    32→
    33→### Prerequisites
    34→
    35→- **Node.js** v22.12.0 or higher (even-numbered versions only)
    36→- **npm**, **pnpm**, or **yarn**
    37→- A code editor (VS Code recommended)
    38→- For Cloudflare deployment: a Cloudflare account (paid plan required for sandboxed plugins, $5/mo)
    39→
    40→### Create a New Site
    41→
    42→```bash
    43→npm create emdash@latest
    44→```
    45→
    46→Follow the prompts to pick a template and name your project. Emdash ships with three official starter templates:
    47→
    48→| Template | Description | Use Case |
    49→|----------|-------------|----------|
    50→| **Blog** | Classic blog with sidebar widgets, search, RSS, categories/tags, dark/light mode | Personal blogs, simple sites |
    51→| **Marketing** | Conversion-focused landing page with hero, features, pricing, FAQ, contact form | Landing pages, SaaS, product marketing |
    52→| **Portfolio** | Visual portfolio with project grid, tag filtering, case study pages | Freelancers, agencies, creatives |
    53→
    54→Each template comes in two variants:
    55→- **Local** (SQLite + filesystem) -- for development and single-server deployments
    56→- **Cloudflare** (D1 + R2) -- for production deployment on Cloudflare Workers
    57→
    58→```bash
    59→# Cloudflare variant example
    60→npm create astro@latest -- --template @emdash-cms/template-marketing-cloudflare
    61→```
    62→
    63→### Start the Development Server
    64→
    65→```bash
    66→cd my-emdash-site
    67→npm install
    68→npm run dev
    69→```
    70→
    71→Open your browser to `http://localhost:4321`.
    72→
    73→### Complete the Setup Wizard
    74→
    75→On first visit to the admin panel, Emdash's Setup Wizard runs automatically:
    76→
    77→1. Navigate to `http://localhost:4321/_emdash/admin`
    78→2. You'll be redirected to the Setup Wizard. Enter:
    79→   - **Site Title** -- your site's name
    80→   - **Tagline** -- a short description
    81→   - **Admin Email** -- your email address
    82→3. Click **Create Site** to register your passkey
    83→4. Your browser will prompt you to create a passkey using Touch ID, Face ID, Windows Hello, or a security key
    84→
    85→Once your passkey is registered, you are logged in and redirected to the admin dashboard.
    86→
    87→> Emdash uses passkey authentication (WebAuthn) instead of passwords. Passkeys are more secure and work with your browser's built-in credential manager. OAuth and magic link fallbacks are also available.
    88→
    89→### Project Structure
    90→
    91→```
    92→my-emdash-site/
    93→├── astro.config.mjs      # Astro + Emdash configuration
    94→├── wrangler.jsonc         # Cloudflare Workers config (if using CF)
    95→├── src/
    96→│   ├── live.config.ts    # Live Collections configuration
    97→│   ├── pages/
    98→│   │   ├── index.astro   # Homepage
    99→│   │   └── posts/
   100→│   │       └── [slug].astro  # Dynamic post pages
   101→│   ├── layouts/
   102→│   │   └── Base.astro    # Base layout
   103→│   └── components/       # Your Astro components
   104→├── .emdash/
   105→│   ├── seed.json         # Template seed file
   106→│   └── types.ts          # Generated TypeScript types
   107→├── seed/                  # Seed data (SQL exports, seed.json)
   108→└── package.json
   109→```
   110→
   111→### Configuration Files
   112→
   113→#### astro.config.mjs
   114→
   115→This is the core configuration file. For a Cloudflare deployment (as used in Shipyard sites):
   116→
   117→```js
   118→// astro.config.mjs
   119→import cloudflare from "@astrojs/cloudflare";
   120→import react from "@astrojs/react";
   121→import { defineConfig } from "astro/config";
   122→import emdash from "emdash/astro";
   123→import { d1, r2 } from "@emdash-cms/cloudflare";
   124→
   125→export default defineConfig({
   126→  site: "https://your-site.example.com",
   127→  output: "server",
   128→  adapter: cloudflare(),
   129→  integrations: [
   130→    react(),
   131→    emdash({
   132→      database: d1({ binding: "DB" }),
   133→      storage: r2({ binding: "MEDIA" }),
   134→    }),
   135→  ],
   136→  devToolbar: { enabled: false },
   137→  vite: { server: { allowedHosts: true } },
   138→});
   139→```
   140→
   141→For local development (SQLite + filesystem):
   142→
   143→```js
   144→import { defineConfig } from "astro/config";
   145→import emdash, { local } from "emdash/astro";
   146→import { sqlite } from "emdash/db";
   147→
   148→export default defineConfig({
   149→  integrations: [
   150→    emdash({
   151→      database: sqlite({ url: "file:./data.db" }),
   152→      storage: local({
   153→        directory: "./uploads",
   154→        baseUrl: "/_emdash/api/media/file",
   155→      }),
   156→    }),
   157→  ],
   158→});
   159→```
   160→
   161→#### src/live.config.ts
   162→
   163→This connects Emdash to Astro's content system:
   164→
   165→```ts
   166→import { defineLiveCollection } from "astro:content";
   167→import { emdashLoader } from "emdash/runtime";
   168→
   169→export const collections = {
   170→  _emdash: defineLiveCollection({ loader: emdashLoader() }),
   171→};
   172→```
   173→
   174→Emdash uses a single `_emdash` collection that internally routes to your content types (posts, pages, products, etc.).
   175→
   176→#### Environment Variables
   177→
   178→```bash
   179→# Required for authentication in production
   180→EMDASH_AUTH_SECRET=your-secret-here
   181→
   182→# Optional: for content preview
   183→EMDASH_PREVIEW_SECRET=your-preview-secret
   184→```
   185→
   186→Generate a secure auth secret:
   187→
   188→```bash
   189→npx emdash auth secret
   190→```
   191→
   192→### Generate TypeScript Types
   193→
   194→For full type safety, generate types from your database schema:
   195→
   196→```bash
   197→npx emdash types
   198→```
   199→
   200→This creates `.emdash/types.ts` with interfaces for all your collections. Your editor will autocomplete field names and catch type errors.
   201→
   202→---
   203→
   204→## 2. Content Management
   205→
   206→### Creating Content
   207→
   208→1. In the admin sidebar, click a collection name (e.g., **Posts**)
   209→2. Click **New Post** (or the equivalent for your collection)
   210→3. Fill in the required fields:
   211→   - **Title** -- the content's display name
   212→   - **Slug** -- URL identifier (auto-generated from title, editable)
   213→4. Add content using the rich text editor
   214→5. Set metadata in the sidebar:
   215→   - **Status** -- Draft, Published, or Archived
   216→   - **Publication date** -- when to publish
   217→   - **Categories and tags** -- taxonomy assignments
   218→6. Click **Save**
   219→
   220→### Content Statuses
   221→
   222→| Status | Visibility | Use Case |
   223→|--------|------------|----------|
   224→| **Draft** | Admin only | Work in progress |
   225→| **Published** | Public | Live content |
   226→| **Archived** | Admin only | Retired content |
   227→
   228→### The Rich Text Editor
   229→
   230→Emdash uses TipTap (ProseMirror) for rich text editing, storing content as Portable Text (structured JSON). The editor supports:
   231→
   232→- **Headings** (H2-H6), **bold**, **italic**, **underline**, **strikethrough**
   233→- **Ordered and unordered lists**
   234→- **Links** (internal and external)
   235→- **Images** from the media library
   236→- **Code blocks** with syntax highlighting
   237→- **Embeds** (YouTube, Vimeo, Twitter)
   238→- **Sections** -- reusable content blocks via `/section` slash command
   239→
   240→#### Slash Commands
   241→
   242→Type `/` in the editor to access quick insert commands:
   243→
   244→| Command | Action |
   245→|---------|--------|
   246→| `/section` | Insert a reusable section |
   247→| `/image` | Insert an image from media library |
   248→| `/code` | Insert a code block |
   249→
   250→#### Keyboard Shortcuts
   251→
   252→| Action | Shortcut |
   253→|--------|----------|
   254→| Bold | `Ctrl/Cmd + B` |
   255→| Italic | `Ctrl/Cmd + I` |
   256→| Link | `Ctrl/Cmd + K` |
   257→| Undo | `Ctrl/Cmd + Z` |
   258→| Redo | `Ctrl/Cmd + Shift + Z` |
   259→| Save | `Ctrl/Cmd + S` |
   260→
   261→### Editing and Revisions
   262→
   263→Changes to published content appear immediately on your site -- no rebuild required. Emdash tracks revision history:
   264→
   265→1. Click **Revisions** in the editor sidebar
   266→2. View previous versions with timestamps
   267→3. Click a revision to preview it
   268→4. Click **Restore** to revert (this creates a new revision, preserving history)
   269→
   270→### Bulk Operations
   271→
   272→1. Use checkboxes to select entries in the content list
   273→2. Click **Bulk Actions** dropdown
   274→3. Select: **Publish**, **Archive**, or **Delete**
   275→4. Confirm the action
   276→
   277→### Searching and Filtering
   278→
   279→- **Search** by title or content (case-insensitive, partial word matching)
   280→- **Filter** by status, date range, author, or taxonomy term
   281→- Click **Clear Filters** to reset
   282→
   283→### Scheduling Content
   284→
   285→1. Set status to **Draft**
   286→2. Set the **Publication date** to a future date and time
   287→3. Save -- content automatically publishes when the date arrives
   288→
   289→### Collections and Content Types
   290→
   291→Emdash uses a **database-first content model**. Collections (content types) are defined in the database, not in code. This means:
   292→
   293→- Non-developers can create and modify content types through the admin UI
   294→- Each collection gets a real SQL table with typed columns (`ec_posts`, `ec_products`, etc.)
   295→- Fields get real SQL columns (not WordPress-style EAV/meta tables)
   296→- Schema changes appear immediately without rebuilds
   297→
   298→Every content table includes system columns automatically:
   299→
   300→| Column | Purpose |
   301→|--------|---------|
   302→| `id` | Primary key |
   303→| `slug` | URL identifier (unique) |
   304→| `status` | draft / published / archived |
   305→| `author_id` | Content author |
   306→| `created_at` | Creation timestamp |
   307→| `updated_at` | Last modified timestamp |
   308→| `published_at` | Publication date |
   309→| `deleted_at` | Soft delete marker |
   310→| `version` | Optimistic locking counter |
   311→
   312→#### Creating a New Collection via Admin UI
   313→
   314→1. Open **Content Types** in the admin panel (admin-only route)
   315→2. Click **Add Collection**
   316→3. Define fields through the visual schema builder
   317→4. Start creating content immediately
   318→
   319→#### Field Types
   320→
   321→| Field Type | Widget | Description |
   322→|------------|--------|-------------|
   323→| `string` | Text input | Single-line text |
   324→| `text` | Textarea | Multi-line plain text |
   325→| `number` | Number input | Numeric values |
   326→| `boolean` | Toggle switch | True/false |
   327→| `datetime` | Date/time picker | Date and time |
   328→| `select` | Dropdown | Single selection |
   329→| `multiSelect` | Multi-select | Multiple selections |
   330→| `portableText` | TipTap editor | Rich text content |
   331→| `image` | Media picker | Image from media library |
   332→| `reference` | Entry picker | Link to another entry |
   333→
   334→### Taxonomies (Categories, Tags, Custom)
   335→
   336→Emdash includes two built-in taxonomies:
   337→
   338→| Taxonomy | Type | Description |
   339→|----------|------|-------------|
   340→| **Categories** | Hierarchical | Nested classification with parent-child relationships |
   341→| **Tags** | Flat | Simple labels without hierarchy |
   342→
   343→#### Managing Terms
   344→
   345→Via the admin dashboard:
   346→1. Go to `/_emdash/admin/taxonomies/category`
   347→2. Enter the term name in the **Add New** form
   348→3. Optionally set slug, parent (for hierarchical), description
   349→4. Click **Add**
   350→
   351→Via the content editor sidebar:
   352→- For categories: check applicable boxes or click **+ Add New**
   353→- For tags: type tag names separated by commas
   354→
   355→Via the API:
   356→```bash
   357→POST /_emdash/api/taxonomies/category/terms
   358→Content-Type: application/json
   359→Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_TOKEN
   360→
   361→{
   362→  "slug": "tutorials",
   363→  "label": "Tutorials",
   364→  "description": "How-to guides and tutorials"
   365→}
   366→```
   367→
   368→#### Custom Taxonomies
   369→
   370→Create taxonomies beyond categories and tags:
   371→
   372→```bash
   373→POST /_emdash/api/taxonomies
   374→Content-Type: application/json
   375→
   376→{
   377→  "name": "genre",
   378→  "label": "Genres",
   379→  "labelSingular": "Genre",
   380→  "hierarchical": true,
   381→  "collections": ["books", "movies"]
   382→}
   383→```
   384→
   385→### Navigation Menus
   386→
   387→Menus are ordered lists of links managed through the admin UI. They support nesting for dropdown navigation.
   388→
   389→Create menus at `/_emdash/admin/menus`.
   390→
   391→Menu item types:
   392→
   393→| Type | Description | URL Resolution |
   394→|------|-------------|----------------|
   395→| `page` | Link to a page | `/{collection}/{slug}` |
   396→| `post` | Link to a post | `/{collection}/{slug}` |
   397→| `taxonomy` | Link to a category/tag | `/{taxonomy}/{slug}` |
   398→| `collection` | Link to a collection archive | `/{collection}/` |
   399→| `custom` | External or custom URL | Used as-is |
   400→
   401→### Widget Areas
   402→
   403→Widget areas are named regions in your templates (sidebars, footer columns, promotional banners) where administrators can place content blocks.
   404→
   405→Widget types:
   406→- **Content widgets** -- rich text stored as Portable Text
   407→- **Menu widgets** -- render a navigation menu
   408→- **Component widgets** -- render a registered component
   409→
   410→### Sections (Reusable Content Blocks)
   411→
   412→Sections are reusable content blocks that editors can insert into any Portable Text field using the `/section` slash command. Common uses: CTAs, testimonials, feature grids, newsletter signups.
   413→
   414→### Media Management
   415→
   416→The media library supports images, documents, videos, and audio files.
   417→
   418→| Category | Supported Extensions |
   419→|----------|---------------------|
   420→| Images | `.jpg`, `.jpeg`, `.png`, `.gif`, `.webp`, `.avif`, `.svg` |
   421→| Documents | `.pdf`, `.doc`, `.docx`, `.xls`, `.xlsx`, `.ppt`, `.pptx` |
   422→| Video | `.mp4`, `.webm`, `.mov` |
   423→| Audio | `.mp3`, `.wav`, `.ogg` |
   424→
   425→**Uploading files:**
   426→- From the media library: click **Upload** or drag-and-drop files
   427→- From the content editor: click the image button and upload inline
   428→- Default max file size: 10MB
   429→
   430→**How uploads work** (R2/S3):
   431→1. Client requests an upload URL from the API
   432→2. Server generates a signed URL with expiration
   433→3. Client uploads directly to storage (bypasses Workers body size limits)
   434→4. Server records file metadata in the database
   435→
   436→**Organizing:** Create folders, search by filename, filter by type/date/folder.
   437→
   438→**Storage backends:**
   439→
   440→| Backend | Configuration | Use Case |
   441→|---------|--------------|----------|
   442→| Local filesystem | `local({ directory: "./uploads" })` | Development |
   443→| Cloudflare R2 | `r2({ binding: "MEDIA" })` | Production on Cloudflare |
   444→| S3-compatible | `s3({ endpoint, bucket, ... })` | AWS, MinIO, etc. |
   445→
   446→**Media providers** (optional, appear as tabs in the media picker):
   447→- **Cloudflare Images** -- automatic optimization, resizing, format conversion
   448→- **Cloudflare Stream** -- video hosting with HLS/DASH adaptive streaming
   449→
   450→### Site Settings
   451→
   452→Global settings managed through the admin under **Settings**:
   453→
   454→```ts
   455→interface SiteSettings {
   456→  title: string;
   457→  tagline?: string;
   458→  logo?: MediaReference;
   459→  favicon?: MediaReference;
   460→  url?: string;
   461→  postsPerPage: number;
   462→  dateFormat: string;
   463→  timezone: string;
   464→  social?: {
   465→    twitter?: string;
   466→    github?: string;
   467→    facebook?: string;
   468→    instagram?: string;
   469→    linkedin?: string;
   470→    youtube?: string;
   471→  };
   472→}
   473→```
   474→
   475→### SEO Metadata
   476→
   477→Emdash supports SEO through:
   478→- Per-page title, description, and OpenGraph images
   479→- Plugin-powered SEO (e.g., `@emdash-cms/plugin-seo` for sitemaps, meta tags, JSON-LD)
   480→- The `<EmDashHead>` component for plugin-contributed `<head>` content
   481→- The `page:metadata` hook for structured metadata contributions
   482→
   483→---
   484→
   485→## 3. Admin Panel
   486→
   487→### Overview
   488→
   489→The admin panel is a React SPA mounted at `/_emdash/admin/`. It uses:
   490→
   491→| Layer | Technology | Purpose |
   492→|-------|-----------|---------|
   493→| Routing | TanStack Router | Type-safe client-side routing |
   494→| Data | TanStack Query | Server state, caching, mutations |
   495→| UI | Kumo (Cloudflare's design system) | Accessible components |
   496→| Tables | TanStack Table | Sorting, filtering, pagination |
   497→| Forms | React Hook Form + Zod | Validation matching server schema |
   498→| Editor | TipTap | Rich text editing (Portable Text) |
   499→| Icons | Phosphor | Consistent iconography |
   500→
   501→### Admin Routes
   502→
   503→| Path | Screen |
   504→|------|--------|
   505→| `/` | Dashboard |
   506→| `/content/:collection` | Content list |
   507→| `/content/:collection/:id` | Content editor |
   508→| `/content/:collection/new` | New entry |
   509→| `/media` | Media library |
   510→| `/content-types` | Schema builder (admin only) |
   511→| `/menus` | Navigation menus |
   512→| `/widgets` | Widget areas |
   513→| `/taxonomies` | Category/tag management |
   514→| `/settings` | Site settings |
   515→| `/plugins/:pluginId/*` | Plugin pages |
   516→
   517→### Manifest-Driven UI
   518→
   519→The admin fetches a manifest from `GET /_emdash/api/manifest` that describes all collections, plugins, and taxonomies. The admin builds its navigation, forms, and editors entirely from this manifest. Schema changes appear immediately without admin rebuild.
   520→
   521→### Role-Based Access
   522→
   523→| Role | Visible Sections |
   524→|------|-----------------|
   525→| **Editor** | Dashboard, assigned collections, media |
   526→| **Admin** | + Content Types, all collections, settings |
   527→| **Developer** | + CLI access, generated types |
   528→
   529→Roles: Administrator, Editor, Author, Contributor.
   530→
   531→### User Management
   532→
   533→Authentication is passkey-first (WebAuthn) with OAuth and magic link fallbacks. Cloudflare Access SSO is also supported for enterprise environments.
   534→
   535→### Import/Export
   536→
   537→- **WordPress migration**: Import posts, pages, media, and taxonomies from WXR exports, WordPress REST API, or WordPress.com
   538→- **Seed files**: Export schema as JSON for version control, import in new environments
   539→- **CLI tools**:
   540→  ```bash
   541→  npx emdash export-seed > seed.json
   542→  npx emdash types
   543→  ```
   544→
   545→### REST API
   546→
   547→All admin operations go through REST APIs at `/_emdash/api/*`:
   548→
   549→**Content:** `GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /api/content/:collection/:id`
   550→**Schema:** `GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /api/schema/collections/:slug`
   551→**Media:** `GET/POST/DELETE /api/media`
   552→**Settings:** `GET/POST /api/settings`
   553→**Menus:** CRUD at `/api/menus/*`
   554→**Taxonomies:** CRUD at `/api/taxonomies/*`
   555→**Plugins:** `/api/admin/plugins/*`
   556→
   557→All list endpoints use cursor-based pagination.
   558→
   559→---
   560→
   561→## 4. Querying Content in Templates
   562→
   563→### Core Query Functions
   564→
   565→```ts
   566→import { getEmDashCollection, getEmDashEntry } from "emdash";
   567→```
   568→
   569→#### Get All Entries
   570→
   571→```astro
   572→---
   573→import { getEmDashCollection } from "emdash";
   574→
   575→const { entries: posts, error } = await getEmDashCollection("posts", {
   576→  status: "published",
   577→  limit: 10,
   578→  where: { category: "news" },
   579→});
   580→---
   581→
   582→<ul>
   583→  {posts.map((post) => (
   584→    <li><a href={`/posts/${post.slug}`}>{post.data.title}</a></li>
   585→  ))}
   586→</ul>
   587→```
   588→
   589→#### Get a Single Entry
   590→
   591→```astro
   592→---
   593→import { getEmDashEntry } from "emdash";
   594→import { PortableText } from "emdash/ui";
   595→
   596→const { slug } = Astro.params;
   597→const { entry: post, error, isPreview } = await getEmDashEntry("posts", slug);
   598→
   599→if (!post) return Astro.redirect("/404");
   600→---
   601→
   602→{isPreview && <div class="preview-banner">Viewing preview</div>}
   603→<h1>{post.data.title}</h1>
   604→<PortableText value={post.data.content} />
   605→```
   606→
   607→### Taxonomy Queries
   608→
   609→```ts
   610→import { getTaxonomyTerms, getTerm, getEntryTerms, getEntriesByTerm } from "emdash";
   611→
   612→// Get all categories (returns tree structure for hierarchical)
   613→const categories = await getTaxonomyTerms("category");
   614→
   615→// Get a single term
   616→const category = await getTerm("category", "news");
   617→
   618→// Get terms for an entry
   619→const postCategories = await getEntryTerms("posts", "post-123", "category");
   620→
   621→// Get entries by term
   622→const newsPosts = await getEntriesByTerm("posts", "category", "news");
   623→```
   624→
   625→### Menu Queries
   626→
   627→```ts
   628→import { getMenu, getMenus } from "emdash";
   629→
   630→const primaryMenu = await getMenu("primary");
   631→// Returns: { id, name, label, items: [{ id, label, url, target, children }] }
   632→
   633→const allMenus = await getMenus();
   634→// Returns: [{ id, name, label }, ...]
   635→```
   636→
   637→### Site Settings
   638→
   639→```ts
   640→import { getSiteSettings, getSiteSetting } from "emdash";
   641→
   642→const settings = await getSiteSettings();
   643→const title = await getSiteSetting("title");
   644→```
   645→
   646→### Widget Areas and Sections
   647→
   648→```ts
   649→import { getWidgetArea } from "emdash";
   650→import { getSection, getSections } from "emdash";
   651→
   652→const sidebar = await getWidgetArea("sidebar");
   653→const cta = await getSection("newsletter-cta");
   654→```
   655→
   656→### Visual Editing
   657→
   658→Every entry includes an `edit` proxy for inline editing annotations:
   659→
   660→```astro
   661→<article {...entry.edit}>
   662→  <h1 {...entry.edit.title}>{entry.data.title}</h1>
   663→  <div {...entry.edit.content}>
   664→    <PortableText value={entry.data.content} />
   665→  </div>
   666→</article>
   667→```
   668→
   669→In edit mode, this enables inline editing via the visual editing toolbar. In production, the proxy produces no output (zero runtime cost).
   670→
   671→### Type-Safe Queries
   672→
   673→```bash
   674→npx emdash types
   675→```
   676→
   677→```ts
   678→import type { Post } from "../.emdash/types";
   679→
   680→const { entries: posts } = await getEmDashCollection<Post>("posts");
   681→const { entry: post } = await getEmDashEntry<Post>("posts", "my-post");
   682→```
   683→
   684→---
   685→
   686→## 5. Deployment
   687→
   688→### Local Development
   689→
   690→```bash
   691→npm run dev
   692→# Opens at http://localhost:4321
   693→# Admin at http://localhost:4321/_emdash/admin
   694→```
   695→
   696→For local dev with SQLite (no Cloudflare account needed):
   697→
   698→```bash
   699→# If using the emdash monorepo demo
   700→pnpm --filter emdash-demo seed
   701→pnpm --filter emdash-demo dev
   702→```
   703→
   704→### Deploying to Cloudflare Workers
   705→
   706→#### Step 1: Create Cloudflare Resources
   707→
   708→```bash
   709→# Install Wrangler CLI
   710→npm install -g wrangler
   711→
   712→# Authenticate
   713→wrangler login
   714→
   715→# Create a D1 database
   716→wrangler d1 create my-site-db
   717→# Note the database_id from the output
   718→
   719→# Create an R2 bucket
   720→wrangler r2 bucket create my-site-media
   721→```
   722→
   723→#### Step 2: Configure wrangler.jsonc
   724→
   725→```jsonc
   726→{
   727→  "$schema": "node_modules/wrangler/config-schema.json",
   728→  "name": "my-emdash-site",
   729→  "compatibility_date": "2026-03-29",
   730→  "compatibility_flags": ["nodejs_compat"],
   731→
   732→  "d1_databases": [
   733→    {
   734→      "binding": "DB",
   735→      "database_name": "my-site-db",
   736→      "database_id": "your-database-id-here"
   737→    }
   738→  ],
   739→
   740→  "r2_buckets": [
   741→    {
   742→      "binding": "MEDIA",
   743→      "bucket_name": "my-site-media"
   744→    }
   745→  ],
   746→
   747→  "observability": { "enabled": true }
   748→}
   749→```
   750→
   751→Here is a real example from the Shipyard Bella's Bistro site:
   752→
   753→```jsonc
   754→{
   755→  "name": "bellas-bistro",
   756→  "compatibility_date": "2026-03-29",
   757→  "compatibility_flags": ["nodejs_compat"],
   758→  "d1_databases": [
   759→    {
   760→      "binding": "DB",
   761→      "database_name": "bellas-bistro",
   762→      "database_id": "9eac689d-5d78-4c9b-8064-2bcb881ba759"
   763→    }
   764→  ],
   765→  "r2_buckets": [
   766→    {
   767→      "binding": "MEDIA",
   768→      "bucket_name": "bellas-bistro-media"
   769→    }
   770→  ],
   771→  "observability": { "enabled": true }
   772→}
   773→```
   774→
   775→#### Step 3: Configure astro.config.mjs
   776→
   777→```js
   778→import cloudflare from "@astrojs/cloudflare";
   779→import react from "@astrojs/react";
   780→import { defineConfig } from "astro/config";
   781→import emdash from "emdash/astro";
   782→import { d1, r2 } from "@emdash-cms/cloudflare";
   783→
   784→export default defineConfig({
   785→  site: "https://your-site.example.com",
   786→  output: "server",
   787→  adapter: cloudflare(),
   788→  integrations: [
   789→    react(),
   790→    emdash({
   791→      database: d1({ binding: "DB" }),
   792→      storage: r2({ binding: "MEDIA" }),
   793→    }),
   794→  ],
   795→});
   796→```
   797→
   798→#### Step 4: Run Database Migrations
   799→
   800→D1 migrations must be run via Wrangler CLI before deployment (DDL statements are not allowed at runtime).
   801→
   802→```bash
   803→# Export and apply the core schema
   804→npx emdash init --database ./data.db
   805→wrangler d1 migrations apply my-site-db
   806→
   807→# Or apply the core schema directly
   808→wrangler d1 execute my-site-db --file=./node_modules/emdash/migrations/0001_core.sql
   809→```
   810→
   811→#### Step 5: Set Secrets
   812→
   813→```bash
   814→npx emdash auth secret
   815→wrangler secret put EMDASH_AUTH_SECRET
   816→wrangler secret put EMDASH_PREVIEW_SECRET
   817→```
   818→
   819→#### Step 6: Deploy
   820→
   821→```bash
   822→wrangler deploy
   823→```
   824→
   825→Your site is now live at `https://my-emdash-site.<your-subdomain>.workers.dev`.
   826→
   827→### Custom Domains
   828→
   829→1. Go to **Workers & Pages** > your worker in the Cloudflare dashboard
   830→2. Click **Custom Domains** > **Add Custom Domain**
   831→3. Follow the DNS setup instructions
   832→
   833→### Public R2 Access (Recommended for Performance)
   834→
   835→1. In the Cloudflare dashboard, go to **R2** > your bucket > **Settings** > **Public access**
   836→2. Enable public access and note the public URL
   837→3. Update your storage config:
   838→
   839→```js
   840→storage: r2({
   841→  binding: "MEDIA",
   842→  publicUrl: "https://pub-xxx.r2.dev"
   843→}),
   844→```
   845→
   846→For production, connect a custom domain to your R2 bucket for better URLs and caching control.
   847→
   848→### D1 Read Replicas
   849→
   850→For globally distributed sites, enable read replication to reduce latency:
   851→
   852→```js
   853→emdash({
   854→  database: d1({
   855→    binding: "DB",
   856→    session: "auto",
   857→  }),
   858→  storage: r2({ binding: "MEDIA" }),
   859→}),
   860→```
   861→
   862→Enable read replication on the D1 database in the Cloudflare dashboard.
   863→
   864→### Preview Deployments
   865→
   866→```bash
   867→wrangler deploy --env preview
   868→```
   869→
   870→Add an environment section to `wrangler.jsonc`:
   871→
   872→```jsonc
   873→{
   874→  "env": {
   875→    "preview": {
   876→      "d1_databases": [
   877→        {
   878→          "binding": "DB",
   879→          "database_name": "my-site-db-preview",
   880→          "database_id": "your-preview-db-id"
   881→        }
   882→      ]
   883→    }
   884→  }
   885→}
   886→```
   887→
   888→### Troubleshooting Deployment
   889→
   890→**"D1 binding not found"** -- Verify the binding name in `wrangler.jsonc` matches your database configuration (`d1({ binding: "DB" })` must match `"binding": "DB"`).
   891→
   892→**"R2 binding not found"** -- Same check for R2 bucket binding names.
   893→
   894→**Migration errors** -- Check migrations were applied with `wrangler d1 migrations list my-site-db`, re-apply if needed.
   895→
   896→---
   897→
   898→## 6. Plugin System
   899→
   900→### Overview
   901→
   902→Emdash plugins extend the CMS without modifying core code. They can:
   903→
   904→- Hook into content lifecycle events (before/after save, delete, publish)
   905→- Store their own data in indexed collections
   906→- Expose settings with auto-generated admin UI
   907→- Add custom admin pages and dashboard widgets
   908→- Create REST API endpoints
   909→- Make HTTP requests with declared host restrictions
   910→
   911→### How Plugins Work
   912→
   913→Every plugin has two parts running in different contexts:
   914→
   915→1. **Plugin descriptor** (`PluginDescriptor`) -- runs at build time in Vite (imported in `astro.config.mjs`). Tells Emdash where to find the plugin and what admin UI it provides.
   916→2. **Plugin definition** (`definePlugin()`) -- runs at request time on the deployed server. Contains runtime logic: hooks, routes, storage.
   917→
   918→### Registering Plugins in astro.config.mjs
   919→
   920→```js
   921→import { defineConfig } from "astro/config";
   922→import emdash from "emdash/astro";
   923→import seoPlugin from "@emdash-cms/plugin-seo";
   924→import auditLogPlugin from "@emdash-cms/plugin-audit-log";
   925→
   926→export default defineConfig({
   927→  integrations: [
   928→    emdash({
   929→      plugins: [
   930→        seoPlugin({ generateSitemap: true }),
   931→        auditLogPlugin({ retentionDays: 90 }),
   932→      ],
   933→    }),
   934→  ],
   935→});
   936→```
   937→
   938→Plugins are resolved at build time. Order matters for hooks with the same priority.
   939→
   940→### Plugin Capabilities
   941→
   942→Capabilities determine what APIs are available in the plugin context. On Cloudflare with sandboxing, these are enforced at runtime. On Node.js, they are advisory.
   943→
   944→| Capability | Grants Access To |
   945→|------------|-----------------|
   946→| `read:content` | `ctx.content.get()`, `ctx.content.list()` |
   947→| `write:content` | `ctx.content.create()`, `ctx.content.update()`, `ctx.content.delete()` |
   948→| `read:media` | `ctx.media.get()`, `ctx.media.list()` |
   949→| `write:media` | `ctx.media.getUploadUrl()`, `ctx.media.upload()`, `ctx.media.delete()` |
   950→| `network:fetch` | `ctx.http.fetch()` (restricted to `allowedHosts`) |
   951→| `network:fetch:any` | `ctx.http.fetch()` (unrestricted) |
   952→| `read:users` | `ctx.users.get()`, `ctx.users.getByEmail()`, `ctx.users.list()` |
   953→| `email:send` | `ctx.email.send()` |
   954→| `page:inject` | Register `page:metadata` / `page:fragments` hooks |
   955→
   956→### Plugin Context
   957→
   958→Every hook and route handler receives a `PluginContext` object:
   959→
   960→| Property | Description |
   961→|----------|-------------|
   962→| `ctx.storage` | Plugin's document collections |
   963→| `ctx.kv` | Key-value store for settings and state |
   964→| `ctx.content` | Read/write site content (capability-gated) |
   965→| `ctx.media` | Read/write media files (capability-gated) |
   966→| `ctx.http` | HTTP client for external requests (capability-gated) |
   967→| `ctx.log` | Structured logger (debug, info, warn, error) |
   968→| `ctx.plugin` | Plugin metadata (id, version) |
   969→| `ctx.site` | Site info: name, url, locale |
   970→| `ctx.users` | User management (capability-gated) |
   971→| `ctx.cron` | Schedule tasks |
   972→| `ctx.email` | Send email (capability-gated) |
   973→
   974→### Available Hooks
   975→
   976→| Hook | Trigger | Return |
   977→|------|---------|--------|
   978→| `plugin:install` | First plugin installation | `void` |
   979→| `plugin:activate` | Plugin enabled | `void` |
   980→| `plugin:deactivate` | Plugin disabled | `void` |
   981→| `plugin:uninstall` | Plugin removed | `void` |
   982→| `content:beforeSave` | Before content save | Modified content or `void` |
   983→| `content:afterSave` | After content save | `void` |
   984→| `content:beforeDelete` | Before content delete | `false` to cancel |
   985→| `content:afterDelete` | After content delete | `void` |
   986→| `content:afterPublish` | After content publish | `void` |
   987→| `content:afterUnpublish` | After content unpublish | `void` |
   988→| `media:beforeUpload` | Before file upload | Modified file info or `void` |
   989→| `media:afterUpload` | After file upload | `void` |
   990→| `page:metadata` | Page render | Metadata contributions |
   991→| `page:fragments` | Page render (trusted only) | HTML/script fragments |
   992→
   993→Hook configuration options: `priority` (execution order, lower = first), `timeout` (ms), `dependencies` (plugin IDs), `errorPolicy` ("abort" or "continue").
   994→
   995→### Sandbox vs. Trusted Execution
   996→
   997→| | Trusted | Sandboxed |
   998→|---|---------|-----------|
   999→| **Runs in** | Main process | Isolated V8 isolate (Dynamic Worker Loader) |
  1000→| **Capabilities** | Advisory (not enforced) | Enforced at runtime |
  1001→| **Resource limits** | None | CPU (50ms), memory (~128MB), subrequests (10), wall-time (30s) |
  1002→| **Network access** | Unrestricted | Blocked; only via `ctx.http` with host allowlist |
  1003→| **Available on** | All platforms | Cloudflare Workers only |
  1004→
  1005→To enable sandboxing, configure `worker_loaders` in `wrangler.jsonc`:
  1006→
  1007→```jsonc
  1008→{
  1009→  "worker_loaders": [{ "binding": "LOADER" }]
  1010→}
  1011→```
  1012→
  1013→> Sandboxed plugins require Cloudflare Workers with paid plan ($5/mo minimum).
  1014→
  1015→### Block Kit (Admin UI for Sandboxed Plugins)
  1016→
  1017→Sandboxed plugins describe their admin UI as JSON using Block Kit. The host renders the blocks -- no plugin-supplied JavaScript runs in the browser.
  1018→
  1019→Block types: `header`, `section`, `divider`, `fields`, `table`, `actions`, `stats`, `form`, `image`, `context`, `columns`.
  1020→
  1021→Element types: `button`, `text_input`, `number_input`, `select`, `toggle`, `secret_input`.
  1022→
  1023→```typescript
  1024→routes: {
  1025→  admin: {
  1026→    handler: async (ctx, { request }) => {
  1027→      const interaction = await request.json();
  1028→      if (interaction.type === "page_load") {
  1029→        return {
  1030→          blocks: [
  1031→            { type: "header", text: "My Plugin Settings" },
  1032→            {
  1033→              type: "form",
  1034→              block_id: "settings",
  1035→              fields: [
  1036→                { type: "text_input", action_id: "api_url", label: "API URL" },
  1037→                { type: "toggle", action_id: "enabled", label: "Enabled", initial_value: true },
  1038→              ],
  1039→              submit: { label: "Save", action_id: "save" },
  1040→            },
  1041→          ],
  1042→        };
  1043→      }
  1044→    },
  1045→  },
  1046→}
  1047→```
  1048→
  1049→### Building a Custom Plugin -- Complete Example
  1050→
  1051→Here is a complete audit log plugin:
  1052→
  1053→```
  1054→my-plugin/
  1055→├── src/
  1056→│   ├── descriptor.ts     # Build-time descriptor
  1057→│   ├── index.ts           # Runtime definition (definePlugin)
  1058→│   └── admin.tsx          # Admin UI (React components) -- optional
  1059→├── package.json
  1060→└── tsconfig.json
  1061→```
  1062→
  1063→**descriptor.ts** (build time):
  1064→
  1065→```typescript
  1066→import type { PluginDescriptor } from "emdash";
  1067→
  1068→export function auditLog(): PluginDescriptor {
  1069→  return {
  1070→    id: "audit-log",
  1071→    version: "0.1.0",
  1072→    entrypoint: "@my-org/plugin-audit-log",
  1073→    adminEntry: "@my-org/plugin-audit-log/admin",
  1074→    adminPages: [{ path: "/history", label: "Audit History", icon: "history" }],
  1075→    adminWidgets: [{ id: "recent-activity", title: "Recent Activity", size: "half" }],
  1076→  };
  1077→}
  1078→```
  1079→
  1080→**index.ts** (runtime):
  1081→
  1082→```typescript
  1083→import { definePlugin } from "emdash";
  1084→
  1085→export function createPlugin() {
  1086→  return definePlugin({
  1087→    id: "audit-log",
  1088→    version: "0.1.0",
  1089→
  1090→    storage: {
  1091→      entries: {
  1092→        indexes: ["timestamp", "action", "collection"],
  1093→      },
  1094→    },
  1095→
  1096→    admin: {
  1097→      settingsSchema: {
  1098→        retentionDays: {
  1099→          type: "number",
  1100→          label: "Retention (days)",
  1101→          default: 90,
  1102→          min: 0,
  1103→          max: 365,
  1104→        },
  1105→      },
  1106→      pages: [{ path: "/history", label: "Audit History", icon: "history" }],
  1107→      widgets: [{ id: "recent-activity", title: "Recent Activity", size: "half" }],
  1108→    },
  1109→
  1110→    hooks: {
  1111→      "plugin:install": async (_event, ctx) => {
  1112→        ctx.log.info("Audit log plugin installed");
  1113→      },
  1114→
  1115→      "content:afterSave": {
  1116→        priority: 200,
  1117→        timeout: 2000,
  1118→        handler: async (event, ctx) => {
  1119→          const entryId = `${Date.now()}-${event.content.id}`;
  1120→          await ctx.storage.entries!.put(entryId, {
  1121→            timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
  1122→            action: event.isNew ? "create" : "update",
  1123→            collection: event.collection,
  1124→            resourceId: event.content.id,
  1125→          });
  1126→        },
  1127→      },
  1128→
  1129→      "content:afterDelete": {
  1130→        priority: 200,
  1131→        handler: async (event, ctx) => {
  1132→          const entryId = `${Date.now()}-${event.id}`;
  1133→          await ctx.storage.entries!.put(entryId, {
  1134→            timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
  1135→            action: "delete",
  1136→            collection: event.collection,
  1137→            resourceId: event.id,
  1138→          });
  1139→        },
  1140→      },
  1141→    },
  1142→
  1143→    routes: {
  1144→      recent: {
  1145→        handler: async (ctx) => {
  1146→          const result = await ctx.storage.entries!.query({
  1147→            orderBy: { timestamp: "desc" },
  1148→            limit: 10,
  1149→          });
  1150→          return { entries: result.items.map((item) => ({ id: item.id, ...item.data })) };
  1151→        },
  1152→      },
  1153→    },
  1154→  });
  1155→}
  1156→
  1157→export default createPlugin;
  1158→```
  1159→
  1160→**package.json** exports:
  1161→
  1162→```json
  1163→{
  1164→  "name": "@my-org/plugin-audit-log",
  1165→  "type": "module",
  1166→  "exports": {
  1167→    ".": { "types": "./dist/index.d.ts", "import": "./dist/index.js" },
  1168→    "./descriptor": { "types": "./dist/descriptor.d.ts", "import": "./dist/descriptor.js" },
  1169→    "./admin": { "types": "./dist/admin.d.ts", "import": "./dist/admin.js" }
  1170→  },
  1171→  "peerDependencies": {
  1172→    "emdash": "^0.1.0",
  1173→    "react": "^18.0.0"
  1174→  }
  1175→}
  1176→```
  1177→
  1178→### Portable Text Block Types (Plugin-Provided)
  1179→
  1180→Plugins can add custom block types to the rich text editor:
  1181→
  1182→```typescript
  1183→admin: {
  1184→  portableTextBlocks: [
  1185→    {
  1186→      type: "youtube",
  1187→      label: "YouTube Video",
  1188→      icon: "video",
  1189→      placeholder: "Paste YouTube URL...",
  1190→      fields: [
  1191→        { type: "text_input", action_id: "id", label: "YouTube URL" },
  1192→        { type: "text_input", action_id: "title", label: "Title" },
  1193→      ],
  1194→    },
  1195→  ],
  1196→}
  1197→```
  1198→
  1199→Site-side rendering uses Astro components exported from `componentsEntry`:
  1200→
  1201→```typescript
  1202→// src/astro/index.ts
  1203→import YouTube from "./YouTube.astro";
  1204→export const blockComponents = { youtube: YouTube };
  1205→```
  1206→
  1207→---
  1208→
  1209→## 7. Theming
  1210→
  1211→### What is an Emdash Theme?
  1212→
  1213→An Emdash theme is a complete Astro site (pages, layouts, components, styles) distributed via `create-astro`. It also includes a **seed file** that bootstraps the database with collections, fields, menus, and sample content on first run.
  1214→
  1215→There is no theme API or abstraction layer. You build a working site and ship it as a template. The seed file tells Emdash what to create.
  1216→
  1217→### Theme Structure
  1218→
  1219→```
  1220→my-emdash-theme/
  1221→├── package.json              # Theme metadata + emdash config
  1222→├── astro.config.mjs          # Astro + Emdash configuration
  1223→├── src/
  1224→│   ├── live.config.ts        # Live Collections setup
  1225→│   ├── pages/
  1226→│   │   ├── index.astro       # Homepage
  1227→│   │   ├── [...slug].astro   # Pages (catch-all)
  1228→│   │   ├── posts/
  1229→│   │   │   ├── index.astro   # Post archive
  1230→│   │   │   └── [slug].astro  # Single post
  1231→│   │   ├── categories/
  1232→│   │   │   └── [slug].astro  # Category archive
  1233→│   │   ├── tags/
  1234→│   │   │   └── [slug].astro  # Tag archive
  1235→│   │   ├── search.astro      # Search page
  1236→│   │   └── 404.astro         # Not found
  1237→│   ├── layouts/
  1238→│   │   └── Base.astro        # Base layout
  1239→│   └── components/
  1240→├── .emdash/
  1241→│   ├── seed.json             # Schema + sample content
  1242→│   └── uploads/              # Optional local media files
  1243→└── public/                   # Static assets
  1244→```
  1245→
  1246→### Key Theme Rules
  1247→
  1248→1. **Theme content pages must be server-rendered** -- do NOT use `getStaticPaths()` in theme content routes
  1249→2. **No hard-coded content** -- site title, tagline, navigation come from the CMS via API calls
  1250→3. **Image fields are objects** -- `post.data.featured_image` is `{ src, alt }`, not a string
  1251→
  1252→### The Seed File
  1253→
  1254→The seed file (`.emdash/seed.json` or `seed/seed.json`) is the heart of a theme. It declares exactly what collections, fields, menus, taxonomies, sections, and sample content the theme needs.
  1255→
  1256→```json
  1257→{
  1258→  "$schema": "https://emdashcms.com/seed.schema.json",
  1259→  "version": "1",
  1260→  "meta": {
  1261→    "name": "My Theme",
  1262→    "description": "A clean theme",
  1263→    "author": "Your Name"
  1264→  },
  1265→  "settings": {
  1266→    "title": "My Site",
  1267→    "tagline": "A tagline",
  1268→    "postsPerPage": 10
  1269→  },
  1270→  "collections": [
  1271→    {
  1272→      "slug": "posts",
  1273→      "label": "Posts",
  1274→      "labelSingular": "Post",
  1275→      "supports": ["drafts", "revisions"],
  1276→      "fields": [
  1277→        { "slug": "title", "label": "Title", "type": "string", "required": true },
  1278→        { "slug": "content", "label": "Content", "type": "portableText" },
  1279→        { "slug": "excerpt", "label": "Excerpt", "type": "text" },
  1280→        { "slug": "featured_image", "label": "Featured Image", "type": "image" }
  1281→      ]
  1282→    },
  1283→    {
  1284→      "slug": "pages",
  1285→      "label": "Pages",
  1286→      "labelSingular": "Page",
  1287→      "supports": ["drafts", "revisions"],
  1288→      "fields": [
  1289→        { "slug": "title", "label": "Title", "type": "string", "required": true },
  1290→        { "slug": "content", "label": "Content", "type": "portableText" }
  1291→      ]
  1292→    }
  1293→  ],
  1294→  "taxonomies": [
  1295→    {
  1296→      "name": "category",
  1297→      "label": "Categories",
  1298→      "hierarchical": true,
  1299→      "collections": ["posts"],
  1300→      "terms": [
  1301→        { "slug": "news", "label": "News" },
  1302→        { "slug": "tutorials", "label": "Tutorials" }
  1303→      ]
  1304→    }
  1305→  ],
  1306→  "menus": [
  1307→    {
  1308→      "name": "primary",
  1309→      "label": "Primary Navigation",
  1310→      "items": [
  1311→        { "type": "custom", "label": "Home", "url": "/" },
  1312→        { "type": "custom", "label": "Blog", "url": "/posts" }
  1313→      ]
  1314→    }
  1315→  ],
  1316→  "sections": [
  1317→    {
  1318→      "slug": "hero-centered",
  1319→      "title": "Centered Hero",
  1320→      "keywords": ["hero", "banner"],
  1321→      "content": [
  1322→        {
  1323→          "_type": "block",
  1324→          "style": "h1",
  1325→          "children": [{ "_type": "span", "text": "Welcome" }]
  1326→        }
  1327→      ]
  1328→    }
  1329→  ],
  1330→  "redirects": [
  1331→    { "source": "/old-path", "destination": "/new-path" }
  1332→  ]
  1333→}
  1334→```
  1335→
  1336→### Page Templates
  1337→
  1338→Add a `template` select field to the pages collection for multiple layouts:
  1339→
  1340→```json
  1341→{
  1342→  "slug": "template",
  1343→  "label": "Page Template",
  1344→  "type": "string",
  1345→  "widget": "select",
  1346→  "options": {
  1347→    "choices": [
  1348→      { "value": "default", "label": "Default" },
  1349→      { "value": "full-width", "label": "Full Width" },
  1350→      { "value": "landing", "label": "Landing Page" }
  1351→    ]
  1352→  },
  1353→  "defaultValue": "default"
  1354→}
  1355→```
  1356→
  1357→Then in the catch-all route:
  1358→
  1359→```astro
  1360→---
  1361→import { getEmDashEntry } from "emdash";
  1362→import PageDefault from "../layouts/PageDefault.astro";
  1363→import PageFullWidth from "../layouts/PageFullWidth.astro";
  1364→import PageLanding from "../layouts/PageLanding.astro";
  1365→
  1366→const { slug } = Astro.params;
  1367→const { entry: page } = await getEmDashEntry("pages", slug!);
  1368→if (!page) return Astro.redirect("/404");
  1369→
  1370→const layouts = {
  1371→  "default": PageDefault,
  1372→  "full-width": PageFullWidth,
  1373→  "landing": PageLanding,
  1374→};
  1375→const Layout = layouts[page.data.template] ?? PageDefault;
  1376→---
  1377→
  1378→<Layout page={page} />
  1379→```
  1380→
  1381→### Custom Portable Text Blocks in Themes
  1382→
  1383→Themes can define custom block types for marketing pages, landing pages, etc.:
  1384→
  1385→```json
  1386→{
  1387→  "_type": "marketing.hero",
  1388→  "headline": "Build something amazing",
  1389→  "subheadline": "The all-in-one platform.",
  1390→  "primaryCta": { "label": "Get Started", "url": "/signup" }
  1391→}
  1392→```
  1393→
  1394→Create Astro components and pass them to the `PortableText` renderer:
  1395→
  1396→```astro
  1397→---
  1398→import { PortableText } from "emdash/ui";
  1399→import Hero from "./blocks/Hero.astro";
  1400→import Features from "./blocks/Features.astro";
  1401→
  1402→const marketingTypes = {
  1403→  "marketing.hero": Hero,
  1404→  "marketing.features": Features,
  1405→};
  1406→---
  1407→
  1408→<PortableText value={value} components={{ types: marketingTypes }} />
  1409→```
  1410→
  1411→### Search
  1412→
  1413→Include search in your theme with the `LiveSearch` component:
  1414→
  1415→```astro
  1416→---
  1417→import LiveSearch from "emdash/ui/search";
  1418→---
  1419→
  1420→<LiveSearch
  1421→  placeholder="Search posts and pages..."
  1422→  collections={["posts", "pages"]}
  1423→/>
  1424→```
  1425→
  1426→Provides debounced instant search with prefix matching, Porter stemming, and highlighted snippets.
  1427→
  1428→### Publishing a Theme
  1429→
  1430→```bash
  1431→# Publish to npm
  1432→npm publish --access public
  1433→
  1434→# Users install with:
  1435→npm create astro@latest -- --template @your-org/emdash-theme-name
  1436→```
  1437→
  1438→For GitHub-hosted themes:
  1439→
  1440→```bash
  1441→npm create astro@latest -- --template github:your-org/emdash-theme-name
  1442→```
  1443→
  1444→---
  1445→
  1446→## 8. Real Examples from Shipyard Sites
  1447→
  1448→### Bella's Bistro (Marketing Template)
  1449→
  1450→**Site URL:** `https://bellas.shipyard.company`
  1451→
  1452→**astro.config.mjs:**
  1453→```js
  1454→import cloudflare from "@astrojs/cloudflare";
  1455→import react from "@astrojs/react";
  1456→import { defineConfig } from "astro/config";
  1457→import emdash from "emdash/astro";
  1458→import { d1, r2 } from "@emdash-cms/cloudflare";
  1459→
  1460→export default defineConfig({
  1461→  site: "https://bellas.shipyard.company",
  1462→  output: "server",
  1463→  adapter: cloudflare(),
  1464→  integrations: [
  1465→    react(),
  1466→    emdash({
  1467→      database: d1({ binding: "DB" }),
  1468→      storage: r2({ binding: "MEDIA" }),
  1469→    }),
  1470→  ],
  1471→  devToolbar: { enabled: false },
  1472→  vite: { server: { allowedHosts: true } },
  1473→});
  1474→```
  1475→
  1476→**wrangler.jsonc:**
  1477→```jsonc
  1478→{
  1479→  "name": "bellas-bistro",
  1480→  "compatibility_date": "2026-03-29",
  1481→  "compatibility_flags": ["nodejs_compat"],
  1482→  "d1_databases": [
  1483→    {
  1484→      "binding": "DB",
  1485→      "database_name": "bellas-bistro",
  1486→      "database_id": "9eac689d-5d78-4c9b-8064-2bcb881ba759"
  1487→    }
  1488→  ],
  1489→  "r2_buckets": [
  1490→    {
  1491→      "binding": "MEDIA",
  1492→      "bucket_name": "bellas-bistro-media"
  1493→    }
  1494→  ],
  1495→  "observability": { "enabled": true }
  1496→}
  1497→```
  1498→
  1499→**Homepage pattern** (`src/pages/index.astro`):
  1500→```astro
  1501→---
  1502→import { getEmDashEntry } from "emdash";
  1503→import Base from "../layouts/Base.astro";
  1504→import MarketingBlocks from "../components/MarketingBlocks.astro";
  1505→
  1506→const { entry: page, cacheHint } = await getEmDashEntry("pages", "home");
  1507→try { Astro.cache.set(cacheHint); } catch {}
  1508→
  1509→const pageTitle = page?.data.title;
  1510→const pageContent = page?.data.content;
  1511→---
  1512→
  1513→<Base title={pageTitle !== "Home" ? pageTitle : undefined}>
  1514→  {pageContent ? (
  1515→    <MarketingBlocks value={pageContent} />
  1516→  ) : (
  1517→    <div class="empty-state">
  1518→      <h1>Welcome</h1>
  1519→      <a href="/_emdash/admin" class="btn btn-primary">Open Admin</a>
  1520→    </div>
  1521→  )}
  1522→</Base>
  1523→```
  1524→
  1525→**Base layout pattern** -- uses `getMenu("primary")` and `getSiteSettings()` for dynamic navigation and site branding, `EmDashHead` for plugin-contributed head content, CSS custom properties for design tokens, and dark/light mode toggle.
  1526→
  1527→**Key scripts:**
  1528→```json
  1529→{
  1530→  "dev": "astro dev",
  1531→  "build": "astro build",
  1532→  "preview": "astro preview",
  1533→  "start": "node ./dist/server/entry.mjs",
  1534→  "bootstrap": "emdash init && emdash seed",
  1535→  "seed": "emdash seed"
  1536→}
  1537→```
  1538→
  1539→### Sunrise Yoga (Marketing Template)
  1540→
  1541→**Site URL:** `https://yoga.shipyard.company`
  1542→
  1543→Follows the same pattern as Bella's Bistro -- same astro.config.mjs structure, same Emdash integration, different D1 database and R2 bucket, different content and styling.
  1544→
  1545→### Common Patterns Across Shipyard Sites
  1546→
  1547→1. **All use Cloudflare deployment** with D1 + R2
  1548→2. **All use the marketing template** as base (`@emdash-cms/template-marketing`)
  1549→3. **Content is page-based** with custom Portable Text marketing blocks (hero, features, pricing, testimonials, FAQ)
  1550→4. **Navigation is menu-driven** via `getMenu("primary")`
  1551→5. **Settings are CMS-driven** via `getSiteSettings()`
  1552→6. **Bootstrap workflow**: `emdash init && emdash seed` to initialize database and apply seed data
  1553→7. **`live.config.ts`** is identical across all sites (single `_emdash` collection with `emdashLoader()`)
  1554→
  1555→---
  1556→
  1557→## Quick Reference
  1558→
  1559→### CLI Commands
  1560→
  1561→| Command | Purpose |
  1562→|---------|---------|
  1563→| `npm create emdash@latest` | Create a new Emdash site |
  1564→| `npm run dev` | Start development server |
  1565→| `npm run build` | Build for production |
  1566→| `npx emdash types` | Generate TypeScript types from DB schema |
  1567→| `npx emdash auth secret` | Generate auth secret |
  1568→| `npx emdash export-seed > seed.json` | Export schema as seed file |
  1569→| `npx emdash init` | Initialize database |
  1570→| `npx emdash seed` | Apply seed data |
  1571→| `wrangler d1 create <name>` | Create D1 database |
  1572→| `wrangler r2 bucket create <name>` | Create R2 bucket |
  1573→| `wrangler d1 migrations apply <db>` | Apply DB migrations |
  1574→| `wrangler secret put <name>` | Set secret |
  1575→| `wrangler deploy` | Deploy to Cloudflare Workers |
  1576→
  1577→### Key Imports
  1578→
  1579→```ts
  1580→// Content queries
  1581→import { getEmDashCollection, getEmDashEntry } from "emdash";
  1582→
  1583→// Taxonomy queries
  1584→import { getTaxonomyTerms, getTerm, getEntryTerms, getEntriesByTerm } from "emdash";
  1585→
  1586→// Navigation and settings
  1587→import { getMenu, getMenus, getSiteSettings, getSiteSetting } from "emdash";
  1588→
  1589→// Widget areas and sections
  1590→import { getWidgetArea } from "emdash";
  1591→import { getSection, getSections } from "emdash";
  1592→
  1593→// UI components
  1594→import { PortableText, EmDashHead, Image } from "emdash/ui";
  1595→import LiveSearch from "emdash/ui/search";
  1596→
  1597→// Page context
  1598→import { createPublicPageContext } from "emdash/page";
  1599→
  1600→// Plugin development
  1601→import { definePlugin } from "emdash";
  1602→
  1603→// Astro integration
  1604→import emdash from "emdash/astro";
  1605→
  1606→// Cloudflare adapters
  1607→import { d1, r2 } from "@emdash-cms/cloudflare";
  1608→
  1609→// Runtime loader
  1610→import { emdashLoader } from "emdash/runtime";
  1611→```
  1612→
  1613→### Admin API Endpoints
  1614→
  1615→| Endpoint | Methods | Purpose |
  1616→|----------|---------|---------|
  1617→| `/_emdash/api/manifest` | GET | Admin manifest (collections, plugins, taxonomies) |
  1618→| `/_emdash/api/content/:collection` | GET, POST | List/create content |
  1619→| `/_emdash/api/content/:collection/:id` | GET, PUT, DELETE | Get/update/delete content |
  1620→| `/_emdash/api/schema/collections` | GET, POST | List/create collections |
  1621→| `/_emdash/api/media` | GET | List media |
  1622→| `/_emdash/api/media/upload-url` | POST | Get signed upload URL |
  1623→| `/_emdash/api/settings` | GET, POST | Get/update site settings |
  1624→| `/_emdash/api/menus` | GET, POST | List/create menus |
  1625→| `/_emdash/api/taxonomies` | GET, POST | List/create taxonomies |
  1626→| `/_emdash/api/taxonomies/:name/terms` | GET, POST | List/create terms |
  1627→
  1628→### Portable Storage Layer
  1629→
  1630→| Layer | Cloudflare | Also works with |
  1631→|-------|-----------|-----------------|
  1632→| Database | D1 | SQLite, Turso/libSQL, PostgreSQL |
  1633→| Storage | R2 | AWS S3, any S3-compatible, local filesystem |
  1634→| Sessions | KV | Redis, file-based |
  1635→| Plugins | Worker isolates (sandboxed) | In-process (trusted mode) |
  1636→
  1637→---
  1638→
  1639→## AI-Managed Content (API Tokens + MCP)
  1640→
  1641→Emdash is designed for AI-native content management. Agents can create, edit, and manage all site content programmatically via Personal Access Tokens (PATs) and the MCP protocol.
  1642→
  1643→### Creating an API Token
  1644→
  1645→1. Log into the Emdash admin panel (`/_emdash/admin`)
  1646→2. Go to **Settings** → **API Tokens**
  1647→3. Click **Create Token**
  1648→4. Set a name (e.g., "AI Agent - Content Creator")
  1649→5. Choose scopes: `content:read`, `content:write`, `media:read`, `media:write`, `schema:read`
  1650→6. Copy the token (starts with `ec_pat_`) — it's only shown once
  1651→
  1652→### Using the REST API
  1653→
  1654→All Emdash API endpoints accept Bearer token authentication:
  1655→
  1656→```bash
  1657→# List all posts
  1658→curl -H "Authorization: Bearer ec_pat_xxxxx" \
  1659→  https://your-site.workers.dev/_emdash/api/content/posts
  1660→
  1661→# Create a post
  1662→curl -X POST \
  1663→  -H "Authorization: Bearer ec_pat_xxxxx" \
  1664→  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  1665→  https://your-site.workers.dev/_emdash/api/content/posts \
  1666→  -d '{"data":{"short_text":"Post Title","long_text":"Post content here"},"status":"published"}'
  1667→
  1668→# Update a post
  1669→curl -X PUT \
  1670→  -H "Authorization: Bearer ec_pat_xxxxx" \
  1671→  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  1672→  https://your-site.workers.dev/_emdash/api/content/posts/POST_ID \
  1673→  -d '{"data":{"short_text":"Updated Title"}}'
  1674→
  1675→# Delete a post
  1676→curl -X DELETE \
  1677→  -H "Authorization: Bearer ec_pat_xxxxx" \
  1678→  https://your-site.workers.dev/_emdash/api/content/posts/POST_ID
  1679→
  1680→# Upload media
  1681→curl -X POST \
  1682→  -H "Authorization: Bearer ec_pat_xxxxx" \
  1683→  -F "file=@image.jpg" \
  1684→  https://your-site.workers.dev/_emdash/api/media
  1685→```
  1686→
  1687→### Available API Endpoints
  1688→
  1689→| Endpoint | Method | Description |
  1690→|----------|--------|-------------|
  1691→| `/_emdash/api/content/{collection}` | GET | List items in a collection |
  1692→| `/_emdash/api/content/{collection}` | POST | Create new item |
  1693→| `/_emdash/api/content/{collection}/{id}` | GET | Get single item |
  1694→| `/_emdash/api/content/{collection}/{id}` | PUT | Update item |
  1695→| `/_emdash/api/content/{collection}/{id}` | DELETE | Delete item |
  1696→| `/_emdash/api/content/{collection}/{id}/publish` | POST | Publish item |
  1697→| `/_emdash/api/content/{collection}/{id}/unpublish` | POST | Unpublish item |
  1698→| `/_emdash/api/media` | GET | List media files |
  1699→| `/_emdash/api/media` | POST | Upload media |
  1700→| `/_emdash/api/media/{id}` | PUT | Update media metadata |
  1701→| `/_emdash/api/media/{id}` | DELETE | Delete media |
  1702→| `/_emdash/api/admin/plugins/{pluginId}/{route}` | POST | Call plugin route |
  1703→| `/_emdash/api/admin/api-tokens` | GET | List API tokens |
  1704→| `/_emdash/api/admin/api-tokens` | POST | Create API token |
  1705→| `/_emdash/api/mcp` | POST | MCP protocol endpoint |
  1706→
  1707→### MCP Server (AI-Native)
  1708→
  1709→Emdash exposes a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for direct AI agent integration:
  1710→
  1711→```
  1712→MCP Endpoint: https://your-site.workers.dev/_emdash/api/mcp
  1713→Authentication: Bearer ec_pat_xxxxx
  1714→```
  1715→
  1716→MCP tools available:
  1717→- Create/read/update/delete content across all collections
  1718→- Upload and manage media files
  1719→- Read schema definitions and content types
  1720→- Manage taxonomies, menus, and settings
  1721→- Full-text search across collections
  1722→- Publish, schedule, and version content
  1723→
  1724→### Token Security
  1725→
  1726→- Tokens are **SHA-256 hashed** in the database — raw tokens are never stored
  1727→- Tokens support **granular scopes** — only grant what the agent needs
  1728→- Tokens track **last_used_at** for security auditing
  1729→- Tokens can have **expiration dates** and **usage limits**
  1730→- All token comparisons use **constant-time comparison** to prevent timing attacks
  1731→
  1732→### Verified Working Example (Sunrise Yoga)
  1733→
  1734→```bash
  1735→# This was tested and confirmed working on 2026-04-08
  1736→curl -H "Authorization: Bearer ec_pat_z3IdW-q-nG4w1bDadqTdGSkUxgNI-FGehCIgRjc2o8Q" \
  1737→  https://sunrise-yoga.seth-a02.workers.dev/_emdash/api/content/posts
  1738→# Returns: { data: { items: [...] } }
  1739→```
